factors.summary: The North American Surface Transportation segment is growing fastest.

evidence.text: NAST revenues increased 18.7 percent to $2.9 billion in the third quarter of 2018 from $2.5 billion in the third quarter of 2017. This increase was primarily driven by increased pricing, including fuel.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 , Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

factors.summary: Management has not explicitly raised or lowered forward guidance, but the discussion suggests confidence in continued growth.

evidence.text: Net income increased 47.6 percent to $175.9 million in the third quarter of 2018 from $119.2 million in the third quarter of 2017. Basic net income per share increased 49.4 percent to $1.27 from $0.85 in the third quarter of 2018 compared to the third quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

factors.summary: Management appears confident, as evidenced by net income growth and increased margins, suggesting a positive outlook.

evidence.text: Net income increased 47.6 percent to $175.9 million in the third quarter of 2018 from $119.2 million in the third quarter of 2017. Basic net income per share increased 49.4 percent to $1.27 from $0.85 in the third quarter of 2018 compared to the third quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

factors.summary: Fuel prices and availability, along with economic conditions like consumer demand and economic recessions, are key sensitivities impacting near-term results.

evidence.text: Risks that could cause actual results to differ materially from our current expectations include changes in economic conditions, including uncertain consumer demand; economic recessions; changes in market demand and pressures on the pricing for our services; fuel prices and availability; changes in the availability of equipment and services from third party providers, including the availability of contracted truckload carriers and changes in prices;...
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS / FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher personnel expenses, including variable compensation plans.

evidence.text: NAST operating expenses increased 13.9 percent during the nine months ended September 30, 2018 to $754.3 million compared to $662.2 million during the nine months ended September 30, 2017. This increase was driven by increases in personnel expenses and, to a lesser extent, other selling, general, and administrative expenses. The increase in personnel expense is related to an increase in expenses related to variable compensation plans.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2017

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, driven by increased earnings.

evidence.text: We generated $528.9 million and $218.3 million of cash flow from operations during the nine months ended September 30, 2018 and September 30, 2017, respectively, an increase of $310.6 million compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2017 driven by increased earnings.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: The company is increasing buybacks and maintaining dividends, signaling confidence.

evidence.text: We used $202.1 million and $130.0 million on share repurchases during the nine months ended September 30, 2018 and September 30, 2017, see Item 2, Part II of this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q. The change was due to an increase in the average price of the repurchased shares and an increase in the number of shares repurchased during the nine months ended September 30, 2018, compared to the same period of 2017. In May 2018, the Board of Directors increased the number of shares authorized for repurchase by 15,000,000 shares.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: The volume trends show mixed performance across different segments. While truckload volumes have declined slightly, LTL and global forwarding services have seen significant growth, contributing to revenue increases.

evidence.text: NAST truckload volumes decreased 0.5 percent in the third quarter of 2018 compared to the third quarter of 2017. Global Forwarding ocean transportation volumes increased approximately seven percent in the third quarter of 2018 compared to the same period of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 , Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: The increase was driven by increased pricing, including fuel, and volume growth in LTL and global forwarding services.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: NAST truckload volumes decreased approximately four percent during the nine months ended September 30, 2018 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2017. NAST LTL net revenues increased 17.4 percent to $338.5 million in the nine months ended September 30, 2018 from $288.3 million in the nine months ended September 30, 2017.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2017

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power with consistent price increases across multiple segments, particularly in the truckload and global forwarding air transportation areas, supported by positive customer responses.

evidence.text: Excluding the estimated impacts of the increase in fuel costs, our average truckload rate per mile charged to our customers increased approximately 14 percent in the third quarter of 2018 compared to the third quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 , Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: Global Forwarding air transportation net revenues increased 17.7 percent to $28.2 million in the third quarter of 2018 from $24.0 million in the third quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: Excluding the estimated impacts of the increase in fuel costs, our average truckload rate per mile charged to our customers increased approximately 22 percent in the nine months ended September 30, 2018 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2017.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: Our truckload transportation costs increased 23 percent, excluding the estimated increase in fuel costs.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2017

factors.summary: The company is experiencing strong organic revenue growth across its transportation and forwarding services, with notable acceleration in the NAST segment and overall transportation revenues.

evidence.text: NAST net revenues increased 23.3 percent to $465.5 million in the third quarter of 2018 from $377.4 million in the third quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 , Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: Global Forwarding net revenues increased 3.3 percent to $134.1 million in the third quarter of 2018 compared to $129.8 million in the third quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: Total transportation revenues increased 17.3 percent to $4.0 billion in the third quarter of 2018 compared to $3.4 billion in the third quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: NAST revenues increased 19.2 percent to $8.5 billion during the nine months ended September 30, 2018 from $7.1 billion during the nine months ended September 30, 2017.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2017

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with acceleration in truckload and LTL segments noted in the third quarter, but a slight decrease in truckload volumes over nine months.

evidence.text: NAST truckload net revenues increased 25.5 percent to $334.7 million in the third quarter of 2018 from $266.6 million in the third quarter of 2017. NAST LTL net revenues increased 19.6 percent to $116.8 million in the third quarter of 2018 from $97.6 million in the third quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 , Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: Total transportation revenues increased 17.3 percent to $4.0 billion in the third quarter of 2018 compared to $3.4 billion in the third quarter of 2017. The increase was driven by increased pricing, including fuel, and volume growth in LTL and global forwarding services.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: NAST truckload volumes decreased approximately four percent during the nine months ended September 30, 2018 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2017. NAST truckload net revenue margin increased slightly in the nine months ended September 30, 2018 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2017.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2017

factors.summary: The revenue mix has shifted towards higher-margin segments, particularly in truckload services such as NAST, contributing to margin expansion and improved profitability.

evidence.text: Our transportation net revenue margin increased to 16.6 percent in the third quarter of 2018 from 16.4 percent in the third quarter of 2017 driven by margin expansion in our truckload services.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: NAST truckload net revenues accounted for approximately 93 percent of our total North American truckload net revenues in the nine months ended September 30, 2018 and 2017. The majority of the remaining North American truckload net revenues are included in Robinson Fresh.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2017

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel, are rising and negatively impacting margins. While the company is attempting to manage these costs through pricing adjustments, it is only partially offsetting the increases.

evidence.text: NAST cost of transportation and related services increased 17.9 percent to $2.5 billion in the third quarter of 2018 from $2.1 billion in the third quarter of 2017, driven by higher cost per mile, including fuel. Global Forwarding costs of transportation and related services increased 19.6 percent to $505.2 million in the third quarter of 2018 from $422.3 million in the third quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 , Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: Total purchased transportation and related services increased 17.1 percent to $3.4 billion in the third quarter of 2018 compared to $2.9 billion the third quarter of 2017. The increase was due to increased cost of transportation, including fuel, and volume growth in LTL and global forwarding services.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: Excluding the estimated impacts of the increase in fuel costs, our average truckload rate per mile charged to our customers increased approximately 22 percent in the nine months ended September 30, 2018 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2017. Our truckload transportation costs increased 23 percent, excluding the estimated increase in fuel costs.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2017

factors.summary: Margins are expanding across both gross and operating metrics, driven by improvements in the NAST segment, particularly in truckload services, and through effective cost management.

evidence.text: NAST net revenues increased 23.3 percent to $465.5 million in the third quarter of 2018 from $377.4 million in the third quarter of 2017. NAST income from operations increased 34.9 percent to $204.2 million in the third quarter of 2018 from $151.4 million in the third quarter of 2017 due primarily to an increase in net revenues.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 , Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: Our transportation net revenue margin increased to 16.6 percent in the third quarter of 2018 from 16.4 percent in the third quarter of 2017 driven by margin expansion in our truckload services. Operating expenses as a percentage of net revenues decreased to 64.6 percent in the third quarter of 2018 from 67.3 percent in the third quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: NAST net revenues increased 18.7 percent to $1.3 billion in the nine months ended September 30, 2018 from $1.1 billion in the nine months ended September 30, 2017. This increase was driven primarily by an increase in truckload and LTL net revenues. NAST income from operations increased 25.8 percent to $562.8 million during the nine months ended September 30, 2018 from $447.6 million in the nine months ended September 30, 2017. This was primarily due to an increase in truckload and LTL net revenues.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2017
